sources.title: KEY LARGO HUMPHREY BOGART
sources.description: Humphrey Bogart plays a former officer returning from WW2 to visit the home of one of his men who perished in the war.

metadata.Plot: Frank McCloud travels to a run-down hotel on Key Largo to honor the memory of a friend who died bravely in his unit during WW II. His friend's widow, Nora Temple, and wheelchair bound father, James Temple manage the hotel and receive him warmly, but the three of them soon find themselves virtual prisoners when the hotel is taken over by a mob of gangsters led by Johnny Rocco who hole up there to await the passing of a hurricane. Mr. Temple strongly reviles Rocco but due to his infirmities can only confront him verbally. Having become disillusioned by the violence of war, Frank is reluctant to act, but Rocco's demeaning treatment of his alcoholic moll, Gaye Dawn, and his complicity in the deaths of the Osceola Brothers and a deputy sheriff start to motivate McCloud to overcome his Hamlet-like inaction.
metadata.Writer: Richard Brooks, John Huston, Maxwell Anderson
metadata.Actors: Humphrey Bogart, Edward G. Robinson, Lauren Bacall
